name: Xxxx-Hot Carribbean Habanero Hot Sauce
recipeIngredient: 4 (up to) Dried; smoked Jalapenos (can use fresh if desired)
recipeInstructions:
1. Hydrate the dried jalapenos by retaining about 1/4 cup of the liquid.
2. Chop all solids and combine with liquid ingredients in a blender.
3. Blend until smooth, ensuring there are no chunks of any ingredient.
- Note: Seeds and flesh of peppers can be left intact for a spicier sauce.
4. Once smooth, cook over very low heat for 30-60 minutes, stirring often.
5. Taste as much as possible and add additional desired ingredients to achieve the desired balance between sweetness and heat.
6. Remove from heat and let cool for 30-60 minutes.
7. Strain the mixture to extract the liquid, discarding seeds, pulp, etc.
8. Store the extracted liquid in an air-tight container or sealed bottle; time will enhance the heat and flavor.
Note: This recipe was created as a total experiment without precise measurements and turned out well (though very spicy). Feel free to adjust and experiment.
